destinations.evidence: The active check is true only when executor is nonzero and expiryBlock is strictly greater than the current block.
Tests document the exact expiry boundary: equal to current block is expired; one before is active.

equivalence_argument: The code and tests align with the strict greater-than rule: missing executor is false, expiry == current is false, expiry < current is false, and expiry > current is true.
notes: This maps both implementation and boundary tests.
rationale: The added boundary tests explicitly align Rust with the contract rule expiryBlock[id] <= block.number, covering missing, active, expired, exact-boundary, and one-before-boundary cases. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: job_exists_and_active must return false for a missing job, true only when jobExecutor is nonzero and expiryBlock is strictly greater than the current block, and false when expiryBlock is equal to or less than the current block.
suggested_falsification_tests: For a job with nonzero executor and expiryBlock 100, assert job_exists_and_active is true at block 99 and false at blocks 100 and 101; also assert a zero executor returns false regardless of expiry.
summary: Active job checks expire at the exact boundary.
test_or_falsification_candidate: For a job with nonzero executor and expiryBlock 100, assert job_exists_and_active is true at block 99 and false at blocks 100 and 101; also assert a zero executor returns false regardless of expiry.
title: Active job check expires at boundary
